frontmatter.title: Campaign Briefs
frontmatter.adaptation_notes:
Sports campaigns: Replace NFL/NBA/MLB with local leagues.
Hashtags: Assess platform usage per market.
Campaign 4 (Luck/Lotteries): Add local lottery references.
Campaign 6 (Game IQ): Quiz uses American odds — adapt format.
Campaign 8 (Big Game): Player copy says "the big game" — never a
trademarked event name. Anchor to your market's marquee event
(championship final, Grand Final, Cup Final) in the timing note only.
Campaign 9 (New Year): Timing assumes January 1 new year. Shift to
local new-year moment where relevant (e.g., Lunar New Year markets).
Campaign 10 (Online Reality Check): Bonus/wagering and RTP publication
rules vary by jurisdiction — verify Myth 19/22/26 claims locally.

campaigns.name: Slots Myths Week
campaigns.theme: Debunking the six most common slot machine misconceptions.
campaigns.tagline: Your slot machine has the emotional range of a toaster.

schedule.copy_source: `myth-busting.md` → Myth 1 → Social card
`myth-busting.md` → Myth 1 → Article explainer
`myth-busting.md` → Myth 2 → Social card
Combined stat highlights from Myths 1 & 2
`myth-busting.md` → Myth 3 → Social card
Combine Myths 3 & 4 into one article: "Your Lucky Machine and the Near-Miss Trap"
`myth-busting.md` → Myth 4 → Social card
`myth-busting.md` → Myth 5 → Social card
`myth-busting.md` → Myth 6 → Social card
"Think you know how slots actually work? Take the quiz." + link

captions.label: Myth 1 post
captions.text: You're on a hot streak. The machine is not. Every spin is independent — your brain sees patterns, the math doesn't. #KnowYourGame

captions.label: Myth 2 post
captions.text: This machine hasn't paid in hours. It's NOT due. Each spin starts from scratch. #NoFinePrint

captions.label: Myth 3 post
captions.text: Your 'lucky machine' doesn't know you. It has the emotional range of a toaster. #KnowYourGame

captions.label: Myth 4 post
captions.text: Two cherries and a blank isn't 'almost winning.' The result was decided before the reels even moved. #NoFinePrint

captions.label: Myth 5 post
captions.text: Slots don't pay more at night. Payout is set in software and regulated. Time of day is irrelevant. #KnowYourGame

captions.label: Myth 6 post
captions.text: Bigger bets = bigger swings. Not better odds. The house edge is a percentage — it doesn't care about your bet size. #NoFinePrint

captions.label: Quiz CTA post
captions.text: You just read 6 slot myths. How many did you already know? Take the Game IQ quiz and find out. Link in bio.

email.subject: The 6 things your slot machine wants you to know (just kidding — it doesn't care)
email.previewText: Every spin is independent. Here's what that actually means.
bodyStructure: Hero: Myth 1 social card image
3 myth/fact pairs (Myths 1, 2, 3 — short versions)
CTA: "See all 6 myths" → links to blog article
Footer: "Test your game IQ" → quiz link

kpis: Social engagement rate (target: 2x baseline for educational content)
Blog article read-through rate
Quiz completions from campaign CTAs
Share/save rate on myth cards

campaigns.name: Beat the House? Table Game Truths
campaigns.theme: Busting the most persistent table game strategies and superstitions.
campaigns.tagline: The dealer follows a script. Literally — it's printed on the felt.

schedule.copy_source: `myth-busting.md` → Myth 7 → Social card
`myth-busting.md` → Myth 7 → Article explainer. Expand with Martingale math table.
`myth-busting.md` → Myth 8 → Social card
`myth-busting.md` → Myth 9 → Social card
`myth-busting.md` → Myth 9 → Article. Title: "Card Counting: What the Movies Got Wrong"
House edge comparison: Blackjack 0.5% vs. Roulette 2.7%/5.26% vs. Baccarat 1.06%
`myth-busting.md` → Myth 10 → Social card
"How well do you know the real odds at the table? Take the quiz."

captions.label: Myth 7 post
captions.text: The Martingale system: double your bet after every loss and you'll always win. Except when table limits exist. And bankrolls are finite. And math is math. #KnowYourGame

captions.label: Myth 8 post
captions.text: Switch tables — that dealer is too hot.' The dealer follows mandatory rules with zero discretion. A 'hot dealer' is just variance with a face. #NoFinePrint

captions.label: Myth 9 post
captions.text: Card counting: real math, terrible movie premise. A skilled counter's edge is 0.5–1.5%. That's thousands of hands for pocket change. #KnowYourGame

captions.label: Myth 10 post
captions.text: Number 17 is not your lucky number. It's a number. On a wheel. With 36 others. All equally likely. #NoFinePrint

email.subject: Your Martingale system called. It has some bad news.
email.previewText: Betting systems, hot dealers, and other table game myths — debunked.
bodyStructure: Hero: Myth 7 card image (Martingale escalation visual)
Myth 7 + Myth 8 as short myth/fact pairs
House edge comparison infographic
CTA: "Read the full breakdown" → blog
CTA: "Take the table games quiz" → quiz

kpis: Social engagement rate on myth cards
Blog read-through (especially Martingale article — expect high interest)
Quiz completion rate
House edge infographic saves/shares

campaigns.name: Sports Betting Reality Check
campaigns.theme: What sports knowledge does and doesn't give you as a bettor.
campaigns.tagline: You know the sport. The sportsbook knows the math.
campaigns.duration: 2 weeks (10 posts). Best timed around a major sporting event or season opener.

schedule.copy_source: `myth-busting.md` → Myth 11 → Social card
Title: "You Know the Sport. Here's Why That Isn't Enough."
"What win rate do you think you need on -110 bets to break even? A) 50% B) 52.4% C) 55%"
`myth-busting.md` → Myth 12 → Social card
`myth-busting.md` → Myth 13 → Social card
Title: "Why Your Favourite Tipster's Record Doesn't Mean What You Think"
Parlay math: how the vig compounds per leg. Visual escalation.
`myth-busting.md` → Myth 14 → Social card
"4 sports betting myths in 4 slides" — one myth/fact per slide
"Think you can beat the book? Test your sports betting IQ."

captions.label: Myth 11 post
captions.text: You've watched every game this season. The sportsbook's algorithms have watched every game ever. And they set the line before you saw it. #KnowYourGame

captions.label: Myth 12 post
captions.text: A 4-leg parlay: 6.25% chance of hitting, ~20% house edge. The payout looks big because the odds are tiny. #NoFinePrint

captions.label: Myth 13 post
captions.text: This tipster is 8 for 10 this week! Cool. With 10,000 tipsters posting, someone's always on a streak right now. That's not skill — it's statistics. #KnowYourGame

captions.label: Myth 14 post
captions.text: You saw the momentum shift. The algorithm saw it 3 seconds ago and moved the line. In-play is exciting. It's not an edge. #NoFinePrint

email.subject: You know the sport. The sportsbook knows the math.
email.previewText: 4 things every sports bettor should understand about how odds actually work.

Myth 11 + Myth 12 as myth/fact pairs
Parlay math infographic
CTA: "Take the sports betting quiz" → quiz
CTA: "Share this with someone who has a 'system'" → social share

kpis: Social engagement rate (expect high on sports content)
Poll participation (Stories)
Quiz completions
Share rate (sports bettors share competitive content)

campaigns.name: Luck, Numbers & Lotteries
campaigns.theme: How pattern-seeking brains misread random events.
campaigns.tagline: The ball doesn't know it's your birthday.

schedule.copy_source: `myth-busting.md` → Myth 15 → Social card
"Number 42 hasn't appeared in 6 months. Is it more likely next draw? Yes / No / Depends"
`myth-busting.md` → Myth 10 → Social card
Combined: "Your Lucky Numbers Aren't Lucky — Here's Why Your Brain Thinks They Are"
`myth-busting.md` → Myth 16 → Social card
"How much do you really know about luck and randomness? Take the quiz."

captions.label: Myth 15 post
captions.text: Number 23 hasn't come up in months. It's overdue, right? Wrong. Lottery balls don't keep track. Every draw starts from scratch. #KnowYourGame

captions.label: Myth 10 post
captions.text: Your lucky number has a 1-in-37 chance. So does every other number. The roulette ball doesn't know it's your birthday. #NoFinePrint

captions.label: Myth 16 post
captions.text: 10 lottery tickets = 10x the chance? Sure. 10 x almost-zero = still almost-zero. You went from 0.000007% to 0.00007%. #KnowYourGame

email.subject: The ball doesn't know it's your birthday
email.previewText: Why your lucky numbers, lucky machines, and lucky feelings aren't actually lucky.

kpis: Poll participation rate
Article read-through
Quiz completions
Social saves (lottery content has high save rates)

campaigns.name: The Money Myths
campaigns.theme: Misconceptions about betting strategy, budgets, and "winning it back."
campaigns.tagline: The house edge doesn't care about your strategy.
audience: especially those who view gambling as a potential income source

schedule.copy_source: `myth-busting.md` → Myth 18 → Social card
Title: "Gambling Is Entertainment. Here's What That Actually Means."
`myth-busting.md` → Myth 17 → Social card
"What does the house edge cost you?" Visual: $100 wagered per game type → expected loss
`myth-busting.md` → Myth 6 → Social card
`myth-busting.md` → Myth 12 → Social card
Title: "Chasing Losses: The Most Expensive Habit in Gambling"
"Know your budget before you play. Set a deposit limit in 10 seconds." + feature CTA

captions.label: Myth 18 post
captions.text: Can you make a living from gambling? A tiny number of people do. They treat it like a job with spreadsheets, bankroll rules, and bad weeks. For 99%+ of players, gambling is entertainment — with a cost. That's fine. Just know which one you are. #KnowYourGame

captions.label: Myth 17 post
captions.text: Down $200? Chasing it means making more bets at a mathematical disadvantage. On average, that digs the hole deeper. Set your limit before you play. #NoFinePrint

captions.label: Myth 6 post
captions.text: Betting max doesn't unlock better odds. A 5% house edge takes 5 cents per dollar whether you bet $1 or $100. Bigger bets = louder volume, not better math. #KnowYourGame

captions.label: Tool promotion post
captions.text: Your entertainment budget is yours to set. Deposit limits take 10 seconds. It's like a seatbelt for your bankroll — set it once and forget it. Link in bio.

email.subject: What does the house edge actually cost you?
email.previewText: We did the math. Here's what $100 wagered looks like across 6 games.

campaigns.name: Game IQ Challenge
campaigns.theme: Social quiz campaign pulling from all myth categories. Focus on shareability and competition.
campaigns.tagline: Think you know the odds? Prove it.
audience: especially socially active and competitive segments

schedule.source: "How well do you really know the odds? We're about to find out. Game IQ quiz drops Wednesday."
Myth 1, 11, 15 quiz questions as Instagram polls. Reveal answer on next slide.
"The Game IQ quiz is live. 10 questions. How sharp are you?" + quiz link
"The average score is 6/10. Think you can beat it? Link in bio."
Post the question with the lowest correct-answer rate. "Only 23% got this right. Can you?"
"Challenge a friend. Share your score. Tag someone who thinks they have a 'system.'"
Top scores, most-missed questions, fun stats. "This week, 4,200 people learned that near misses aren't real."

campaigns.name: Know Your Game
campaigns.theme: Game education — teach players how the games they love actually work.
audience: all player segments — particularly new-to-game and casual players

schedule.content: Launch: "Know Your Game — how much do you really know?"
Slots: What RTP and volatility mean for your play
Slots: The most common bet types explained
Slots quiz: 3 questions to test your knowledge
Blackjack: Every move explained — hit, stand, split, double
Roulette: Inside bets vs. outside bets
Roulette: The math behind the wheel (house edge deep dive)
Sports betting: How odds and vig work
Sports betting: Bet types from spreads to parlays
Capstone: Odds comparison across all 4 games + wrap-up email

kpis: Blog traffic from social cards (primary metric)
Social engagement rate on game-specific cards
Email open + click-through rates
Quiz completion rates (Days 4, 7, 10, 13)
Cross-game engagement (players who engage with 3+ game posts)

campaigns.name: The Big Game, By the Numbers
campaigns.theme: A 7-day countdown to the year's biggest game — the one week when casual fans place their first-ever bet.
campaigns.tagline: The biggest game of the year deserves your sharpest bet.
campaigns.duration: 1 week (7 posts), ending on game day

schedule.copy_source: "6 days to the big game. Before the hype: here's how the lines actually work." (adapts SE-1, `core-messages.md`)
Title: "Props, Squares, and Parlays: A Field Guide to Big-Game Bets" — what each bet is, what it costs
`myth-busting.md` → Myth 11 → Social card
What a hype parlay actually costs — vig compounding per leg
`myth-busting.md` → Myth 25 → Social card. Frame: everyone posting winning slips this week is the highlight reel.
"Set your game-day budget before kickoff. 10 seconds now, zero math during the game."
Live-betting reality check: the algorithm moves faster than the broadcast

captions.label: Countdown post
captions.text: 6 days out. The squares pool is filling up and someone in the group chat has a 9-leg parlay. Before kickoff, here's how the lines actually work. #KnowYourGame

captions.label: Myth 11 post
captions.text: You've watched every snap this season. The sportsbook's models have watched every snap ever, and they set the line before you opened the app. Bet for fun, not for certainty. #KnowYourGame

captions.label: Myth 25 post
captions.text: Your feed is full of winning slips this week. That's the highlight reel, not the average. Nobody screenshots the parlay that died on leg two. #NoFinePrint

captions.label: Tool promotion post
captions.text: Game-day plan: snacks, the good TV, and a budget you set before kickoff. 10 seconds now means zero math during the fourth quarter. Link in bio.

captions.label: Game-day post
captions.text: Momentum shifted? The algorithm priced it before the replay ended. In-play betting is exciting, but it's not an edge. Enjoy the game. #KnowYourGame

email.subject: The big game, by the numbers
email.previewText: Props, parlays, and squares: what this week's bets actually cost.

campaigns.name: New Year, Same Math
campaigns.theme: Fresh-start energy, pointed at the one resolution that actually works: set your limits while you're feeling sharp.
campaigns.tagline: New year, same math. New you, better plays.
campaigns.duration: 1 week (6 posts), spanning year-end through the first week of January

schedule.copy_source: "The math didn't change this year. The house edge was the house edge. Here's the refresher."
Title: "The Only Gambling Resolution That Works" — skip 'win more,' set a budget instead
NY-1 from `core-messages.md`: "New year, same math. But here's a resolution that actually works..."
NY-2 from `core-messages.md`: "Start the year with your limits set and your tools active. It takes 30 seconds."
See email option below
"Start the year knowing the odds. 10 questions, 2 minutes."

captions.label: Year-in-review post
captions.text: Slot payouts on December 31: set in software. Slot payouts on January 1: same software. The math doesn't do resolutions. You can. #NoFinePrint

captions.label: Resolution post
captions.text: Most resolutions fail by February. This one takes 30 seconds and runs itself: set your entertainment budget before you play. #KnowYourGame

captions.label: Tool promotion post
captions.text: New year's day checklist: leftovers, couch, and limits set while you're feeling sharp. Future you says thanks. Link in bio.

captions.label: Quiz CTA post
captions.text: New year, new Game IQ. 10 questions on the math behind the games you play. Start the year sharp. Link in bio.

email.subject: New year. Same math. Better plays.
email.previewText: Your year in play, plus the one resolution that runs itself.

campaigns.name: Online Reality Check
campaigns.theme: The algorithm isn't out to get you — how online games actually work, from independent testing to bonus math.
campaigns.tagline: The game doesn't know you. That's the whole point.

schedule.copy_source: `myth-busting.md` → Myth 19 → Social card
Combined: "Is Online Rigged? Inside the Testing Labs, the Audits, and Your 'Lucky New Account'"
`myth-busting.md` → Myth 20 → Social card
Myth 24 STAT line: "Turbo doubles your spins per hour. Same odds, twice the spend rate."
`myth-busting.md` → Myth 21 → Social card
Week 1 recap: three myths, three facts, one CTA
`myth-busting.md` → Myth 22 → Social card
Title: "Bonus Math, Decoded: What 30x Wagering Actually Means" — include the $100/30x worked example
`myth-busting.md` → Myth 24 → Social card
`myth-busting.md` → Myth 25 → Social card
Myth 26 short version: demo and real money run the same certified math
"Think the algorithm is out to get you? Take the online reality check."

captions.label: Myth 19 post
captions.text: Online slots are tested by independent labs, and unlike the casino floor, most show their payback rate right in the game info. The edge is real. The conspiracy isn't. #NoFinePrint

captions.label: Myth 20 post
captions.text: The game wasn't 'about to pay out' when you walked away. The result is generated the instant you press spin. Everything else is animation. #KnowYourGame

captions.label: Myth 21 post
captions.text: There's no 'new player luck' setting and no winner flag. One game, one payout rate, every account. Your early wins were a lucky run, not bait. #NoFinePrint

captions.label: Myth 22 post
captions.text: A $100 bonus at 30x wagering = $3,000 in bets before you can withdraw. Bonuses can be good value, but read the multiplier before you celebrate. #KnowYourGame

captions.label: Myth 24 post
captions.text: Auto-spin doesn't change your odds. It changes your speed, which changes how fast your budget meets the house edge. The only setting that matters is pace. #KnowYourGame

captions.label: Myth 25 post
captions.text: Streamers run 10,000+ spins a week, often on sponsored balances, and you see the highlights. That's a TV show, not a strategy. #NoFinePrint

captions.label: Quiz CTA post
captions.text: Eight online myths, busted in two weeks. How many had you? Take the online reality check. 2 minutes. Link in bio.

email.subject: The game doesn't know you. That's the whole point.
email.previewText: Independent testing, published payouts, and why your "lucky new account" was just math.
bodyStructure: Hero: Myth 19 card image
3 myth/fact pairs (Myths 19, 20, 21 — short versions)
"Bonus math, decoded" teaser → Week 2 blog article
CTA: "Take the online reality check" → quiz link

kpis: Social engagement rate (rigged/conspiracy content generates discussion — expect high comments)
In-app push and popup click-through rates (first campaign benchmark for this channel)
Blog read-through on the bonus math article
Quiz completions and share rate
